metadata.title: China Macro Circulation Model, 2023
metadata.subtitle: An actor-centered flow-of-funds graph for the red-blood-cell metaphor
metadata.node_rule: Nodes are economic actors or process nodes.
metadata.edge_rule: Edges are economic relationships: payments, taxes, transfers, deposits, loans, bonds, and investment flows.
metadata.important_caveat: This graph shows sectoral macro-accounting flows, not literal transaction-level money paths.
source_tables: 2023年中国资金流量表（非金融交易）

manual.description: Editable/story layer. Tweak labels, copy, layout, scenes, and visibility here or in the script.

nodes.label: Households
nodes.short_explanation: People and families: receive income, consume, save, deposit, and borrow.

nodes.label: Firms / Producers
nodes.label_cn: 非金融企业 / 生产者
nodes.short_explanation: Nonfinancial firms: produce goods/services, pay wages and taxes, borrow, and invest.

nodes.label: Financial Institutions / Banks
nodes.short_explanation: The financial transformer: receives deposits and other liabilities, acquires loans and securities as assets.

nodes.label: Government
nodes.short_explanation: The fiscal valve: taxes, transfers, public consumption, public investment, and bond financing.

nodes.label: Rest of World
nodes.short_explanation: External demand and cross-border income/financial flows.

nodes.label: Production Engine
nodes.short_explanation: Not a wallet: a process that turns labor, capital, and demand into future income.

edge_specs.label: Wages from firms
edge_specs.plain_english: Firms pay labor income to households. This is a main pipe from production to household purchasing power.
edge_specs.jargon: Recorded as compensation of employees paid by nonfinancial enterprises.

edge_specs.label: Finance-sector wages
edge_specs.plain_english: Financial institutions also pay workers, adding to household income.
edge_specs.jargon: Recorded as compensation of employees paid by financial institutions.

edge_specs.label: Public-sector wages
edge_specs.plain_english: Government pays wages to public-sector workers, moving public funds into household income.
edge_specs.jargon: Recorded as compensation of employees paid by general government.

edge_specs.label: Household consumption
edge_specs.plain_english: Households use income to buy goods and services. This is the main oxygen-delivery path for domestic demand.
edge_specs.jargon: The official value is household actual final consumption. It is drawn as Households → Firms for teaching clarity.
edge_specs.caveat: The table records a household use, not a literal counterparty matrix.

edge_specs.label: Public consumption / procurement
edge_specs.plain_english: Government spending on final consumption supports demand for goods and services.
edge_specs.jargon: Recorded as government actual final consumption.
edge_specs.caveat: Drawn as Government → Firms for teaching clarity.

edge_specs.label: Foreign demand / net exports
edge_specs.plain_english: External demand enters the domestic circulation through net exports. Goods flow outward; payment/demand flows inward.
edge_specs.jargon: Net exports are exports minus imports. The graph uses absolute value and money-flow direction for teaching.
edge_specs.caveat: This is net exports, not gross exports; it is not a full export-dependence measure.

edge_specs.label: Taxes & social contributions
edge_specs.plain_english: Households send money into the fiscal/social system through taxes, social contributions, and other current transfers.
edge_specs.jargon: Grouped current transfers paid by households, including income/property taxes and social insurance contributions.

edge_specs.label: Production taxes
edge_specs.plain_english: Firms pay net production taxes to government as part of the production process.
edge_specs.jargon: Recorded as net taxes on production paid by nonfinancial enterprises.

edge_specs.label: Social benefits & transfers
edge_specs.plain_english: Government moves money back toward households through social benefits and transfers.
edge_specs.jargon: Recorded as current transfers paid by general government. Drawn toward households for teaching clarity.

edge_specs.label: Services in kind
edge_specs.plain_english: Government provides services to households, such as public services consumed by households even if not paid as cash.
edge_specs.jargon: Social transfers in kind are not ordinary cash payments; render separately or with a distinct style.

edge_specs.label: Deposits
edge_specs.plain_english: Households place a large share of new financial assets into deposits. This is the main clotting cue.
edge_specs.jargon: In the financial account, household deposits are net acquisition of deposit assets.

edge_specs.label: Insurance reserves
edge_specs.plain_english: Some household saving enters insurance-reserve-like financial claims.
edge_specs.jargon: Recorded as household net acquisition of insurance technical reserves.

edge_specs.label: Enterprise deposits
edge_specs.plain_english: Firms also increase deposits, storing some money in the financial system.
edge_specs.jargon: Recorded as nonfinancial corporations' net acquisition of deposits.

edge_specs.label: Government deposits
edge_specs.plain_english: Government also holds/increases deposits, but in this flow view it is still a net borrower overall.
edge_specs.jargon: Recorded as general government net acquisition of deposits.

edge_specs.label: Household loans
edge_specs.plain_english: Households borrow, receiving credit but also taking on liabilities.
edge_specs.jargon: Recorded as household net incurrence of loan liabilities. Drawn from the financial system for teaching clarity.

edge_specs.label: Firm loans
edge_specs.plain_english: Firms rely heavily on loans to finance activity and investment.
edge_specs.jargon: Recorded as nonfinancial corporations' net incurrence of loan liabilities.

edge_specs.label: Government loans
edge_specs.plain_english: Government loan borrowing exists but is much smaller than bond financing in this dataset.
edge_specs.jargon: Recorded as general government net incurrence of loan liabilities.

edge_specs.label: Government bonds
edge_specs.plain_english: Government mainly borrows through bonds in this flow view.
edge_specs.jargon: Recorded as general government net incurrence of debt securities liabilities. Drawn from financial institutions for teaching clarity.

edge_specs.label: Corporate bonds
edge_specs.plain_english: Firms also borrow through bonds, though this flow is much smaller than loans in 2023.
edge_specs.jargon: Recorded as nonfinancial corporations' net incurrence of debt securities liabilities.

edge_specs.label: Business investment
edge_specs.plain_english: Firms invest in capital formation. This can support future production, but it is not the same as immediate household consumption.
edge_specs.jargon: Recorded as nonfinancial enterprise gross capital formation.

edge_specs.label: Public investment
edge_specs.plain_english: Government investment can support future production and public infrastructure.
edge_specs.jargon: Recorded as general government gross capital formation.

edge_specs.label: Household capital formation
edge_specs.plain_english: Households also have capital formation, often visually useful as a housing-like investment channel.
edge_specs.jargon: Recorded as household gross capital formation.

edge_specs.label: Future income loop
edge_specs.plain_english: Investment may support future production and income, but this table does not prove the quality or timing of that return.
edge_specs.jargon: Conceptual feedback edge, not an official measured flow.
edge_specs.caveat: Render dashed and do not scale alongside official flows.

edge_categories.goods_and_services: Consumption, public consumption, and external demand.
edge_categories.fiscal: Taxes, social contributions, transfers, subsidies, and public redistribution.
edge_categories.saving_and_deposits: Deposits, insurance reserves, and financial saving flows.
edge_categories.securities: Bond financing and other debt securities.
edge_categories.investment: Capital formation and productive investment channels.
edge_categories.conceptual: Dashed explanatory links, not directly measured official flows.

compensation_of_employees.label: Compensation of employees
compensation_of_employees.plain_english: Wages and labor income paid to workers.
compensation_of_employees.why_it_matters: One of the main ways production becomes household income.

actual_final_consumption.label: Actual final consumption
actual_final_consumption.plain_english: Goods and services actually consumed by households or government.
actual_final_consumption.why_it_matters: The main oxygen-delivery path in the red-blood-cell metaphor.

gross_saving.label: Gross saving
gross_saving.plain_english: Income not used for current consumption after relevant accounting adjustments.
gross_saving.why_it_matters: High saving can fund investment, but can also signal weak consumption circulation.

deposits.label: Deposits
deposits.why_it_matters: Deposits are safe storage; large deposit accumulation can represent clotting.

loans.label: Loans
loans.plain_english: Borrowed funds: liabilities for borrowers and assets for lenders.
loans.why_it_matters: Loans recirculate saved money into households, firms, and government.

debt_securities.label: Debt securities / bonds
debt_securities.plain_english: Borrowing through bond issuance rather than direct loans.
debt_securities.why_it_matters: In this graph, government financing is heavily bond-based.

net_exports.label: Net exports
net_exports.plain_english: Exports minus imports. In the money-flow view, positive net export demand is drawn as payment from the rest of the world into domestic producers.
net_exports.why_it_matters: It shows the external-demand pipe in the circulation model.

net_financial_investment.label: Net financial investment
net_financial_investment.plain_english: Positive means net lending/surplus; negative means net borrowing/financing need.
net_financial_investment.why_it_matters: A compact way to show which sectors supply funds and which need financing.

scenes.title: 1. Domestic demand loop
scenes.description: Firms pay income to households; households return demand through consumption.

scenes.title: 2. Savings and clotting
scenes.description: A large household flow enters deposits and insurance reserves rather than immediately returning as consumption.

scenes.title: 3. Credit recirculation
scenes.description: The financial system channels funds into loans and bonds for firms, households, and government.

scenes.title: 4. Government as fiscal valve
scenes.description: Taxes and contributions enter government; transfers and public spending flow back out.

scenes.title: 5. External demand
scenes.description: The rest of the world supplies demand through net exports. Goods flow outward; payment/demand flows inward.

scenes.title: 6. Investment and future income
scenes.description: Investment is not a dead end. It may create future production and income, but its return quality is not proven by this flow table.

scenes.title: 7. Red blood cell diagnosis
scenes.description: Healthy oxygen delivery, clotting, recirculation, debt dependence, and external oxygen are highlighted together.

calculated.description: Automatically calculated from the sector-tree JSON files. Do not hand-edit unless you know you are overriding source data.

health.plain_english: Households consume a large amount, but also save heavily. New household financial assets are concentrated in deposits, especially time deposits.
Firms generate most value added and pay much labor income, but investment exceeds their own saving, so they rely heavily on borrowing.
In this flow view, government is not a hoarding node: it has negative saving and net borrowing, financed mainly through bonds.
The financial system transforms deposits and other liabilities into loans and securities. This is where saved money may re-enter production or loop through finance/debt.
Rest of World represents external demand and cross-border income. Net exports are drawn as money/demand flowing from foreign buyers into domestic producers.
Production is not a wallet. It is the engine turning labor, capital, and demand into income.

explain.caveat: This is net exports, not gross exports; it is not a full export-dependence measure.

oxygen_delivery.definition: Money reaches final demand and supports producer revenue.

clotting.definition: Money pools as deposits or low-velocity financial storage.

recirculation.definition: The financial system turns deposits and liabilities into credit and securities.

empty_spinning.definition: Funds circulate through investment/credit loops without necessarily becoming household consumption quickly.
empty_spinning.caveat: This is an interpretive diagnostic, not an official statistical category.

external_oxygen.definition: External demand enters the domestic circulation through the Rest of World node.
external_oxygen.caveat: Uses net exports, not gross exports; not a complete export-reliance indicator.

debt_absorption.definition: Future income is absorbed by repayment or debt service.
debt_absorption.caveat: Debt service is not cleanly measured as a direct edge in the current flow tree files. Use as annotation only.

graph.description: Merged D3-ready graph. D3 can read this directly.

validation.source_tree_validation_note: Run validate_fof_trees.py separately; expected current result is hard failures = 0, with bridge discrepancies as warnings.

evidence_levels.direct_official_flow: Directly read from one official table item.
evidence_levels.direct_official_flow_grouped: Directly read from an official parent item; child breakdown included when available.
evidence_levels.direct_official_flow_simplified_counterp: Official value, but displayed as actor-to-actor edge for teaching clarity.
evidence_levels.direct_official_flow_simplified_lender: Official borrower-side liability flow; lender is simplified as financial institutions.
evidence_levels.direct_official_flow_simplified_holder: Official liability/security flow; holder is simplified as financial institutions.
evidence_levels.direct_official_flow_simplified_process: Official flow displayed as actor-to-process edge for teaching clarity.
evidence_levels.direct_official_flow_sign_normalized: Official flow whose sign/direction is normalized for money-flow visualization.
evidence_levels.direct_official_flow_non_cash_service: Official flow that may represent services in kind rather than ordinary cash payments.
evidence_levels.conceptual_only: Used for explanation; not a measured official flow.
